name: netdust-agent
description: Netdust two-layer agent harness — a SKELETON layer (steps, gates, ordering: harnessed-development entry sequencer + the gates it fires: testing-workflow, threat-modeling, architecture-invariants, feature-acceptance, test-effectiveness, shake-out, compounding, standards-gate; with writing-plans + finishing-a-branch deferred to their superpowers bases and the process retro to the /evaluate command) and a CRAFT layer (gerund-named how-to the harness reaches for at each step: writing-tests, designing-apis, building-frontend, engineering-context, versioning-with-git, driving-the-browser, refining-ideas, sourcing-from-docs, doubting-decisions, simplifying-code, deploying; plus the dev-stack infra skill). Naming convention: gerund = craft (how), phase/gate noun = harness (when/whether). Optional spec-kit graft: gate-bearing spec/plan/tasks override templates + deterministic gate-check.py + per-project installer in spec-kit/, the spec-authoring/spec-analysis/constitution-bridge skills, and /spec-kit-setup; SubagentStop also backstops the standards gate — keystone: the handoff is tasks.md, /speckit.implement is never run (see docs/spec-kit-integration-adr.md). Built on superpowers (base) + addyosmani/agent-skills (craft fills, MIT) with Netdust discipline as the authoritative spine. Standalone and self-contained — the full harness, specialist reviewer agents, hooks, and commands all live here, with no dependency on any other Netdust plugin.
